reasoning: As of late 2025, there is no approved vaccine for Prevotella infections. Recent biomedical literature only features preliminary in silico (computational) studies proposing multi-epitope vaccine designs against Prevotella species. Advancing from computer modeling to an actual, proven biological preparation that provides active immunity requires years of preclinical and clinical testing. It is biologically and logistically impossible for a Prevotella vaccine to be developed, proven, and recognized on Wikipedia within the 1- to 3-month forecast horizon. The most recent known status on the Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' page (as of October 2025) is 'No', and there are no developments that would legitimately alter this. The only way the question could resolve as 'Yes' is via an erroneous or vandalized Wikipedia edit that goes unnoticed on the resolution date. This is highly unlikely, so the probability remains near the minimum possible threshold. The probability slightly strictly increases over time to account for the growing cumulative window in which an errant edit might occasionally appear and persist.
